title: Add query.Roadmap epic-chain computation to internal/query
description:
Foundation tick for phase 4 (spec: docs/design/execution-model-v2-epic-chaining-and-learning-loop.md §1 tk-changes bullet 2-3). The tk roadmap command, the TUI roadmap view, and the tickboard roadmap endpoint will ALL consume this — design for those consumers.

New files: internal/query/roadmap.go + roadmap_test.go (style of plan.go/ready.go).

Compute an epic-level dependency view over all ticks:
- Nodes: every epic (any status). Edges: BlockedBy entries that point at other epics (ignore blockers pointing at tasks for chain purposes, but missing blockers = closed, same as ready.go).
- Group into waves with Kahn-style layering like internal/wave does for tasks (do NOT import or modify internal/wave — it is task-scoped; reimplement at epic level here).
- Per epic emit a consumer-facing status (what a UI should SHOW, not internal predicates):
  done (closed) | active (in_progress, or open with open children) | ready (open, unblocked, has no open children but unstarted: i.e. needs planning per EpicsNeedingPlanning, reuse it) | queued (open but blocked) | gated (open + IsAwaitingHuman — carry the awaiting type for a badge).
- Output type: Roadmap struct { Waves [][]RoadmapEpic } where RoadmapEpic has ID, Title, Status (string enum above), AwaitingType, BlockedBy []string (epic ids only), ChildrenTotal, ChildrenClosed ints (for progress display).

Test cases (table-driven): linear 3-epic chain (closed, in_progress, blocked) -> 3 waves, statuses done/active/queued; childless unblocked epic -> ready; gated epic -> gated with awaiting type; diamond dependency -> correct waves; epic blocked by missing id -> treated unblocked; progress counts correct with mixed-status children.

Run: go test ./internal/query/... && go vet ./...
acceptance_criteria: go test ./internal/query/... passes with new table-driven tests; go vet clean; exported types document the status enum
closed_reason: ComputeRoadmap merged: 18 table-driven tests, status enum done|active|ready|queued|gated, deterministic wave ordering
